product_updates.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
product_updates.content: At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.

product_updates.title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become
product_updates.content: Just for kicks, I took a look at Jersey Mike's IPO documents. Surely a sandwich shop would have no need to mention AI. But lo and behold.

product_updates.title: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
product_updates.content: Meta has quietly launched Pocket, an experimental AI app that lets users generate and share interactive mini games using text prompts.

product_updates.title: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung
product_updates.content: The news comes about a week after OpenAI announced its own custom AI chip in a partnership with Broadcom.

product_updates.title: OpenAI proposed donating 5% of its equity to a US sovereign wealth fund
product_updates.content: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reportedly proposed giving 5% of the company’s equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, reviving discussions about letting the public share in the financial gains from the AI boom.

product_updates.title: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5 billion commitment
product_updates.content: Microsoft follows Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic with its new AI deployment group.

product_updates.title: Yep, we’re using OpenClaw to date now
product_updates.content: Ben Guez has "a bunch of potential international wives in [his] DMs," thanks to an automated script he set up using OpenClaw, Claude code, and Instagram trials.

product_updates.title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30M of his own money to build AI alternative to Microsoft Office
product_updates.content: Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office and Google Apps with AI.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Newly discovered PamStealer isn't your typical macOS malware
technical_breakthroughs.content: The discovery underscores the increased effort being poured into Mac infostealers.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Achieving operational excellence with AI
technical_breakthroughs.content: Frameworks like Lean Six Sigma and business process management (BPM) first gained traction because they promised clarity in the chaos—a structured way to bring order to messy, sprawling operations. Lean Six Sigma emphasized statistical rigor and quality control; BPM created end-to-end maps of how…

technical_breakthroughs.title: Teaching AI to run with the turbines
technical_breakthroughs.content: Artificial intelligence may have captured the public imagination through chatbots and image generators, but some of its most consequential use cases are unfolding far from consumer-facing tools. technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: a startup has a solution for AI’s groupthink problem
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. technical_breakthroughs.title: Why California’s carbon manure math doesn’t add up
technical_breakthroughs.content: Something stinks in California’s climate policies. Years ago, the state set up a system that pays cattle farmers across the country to turn the methane emitted from cattle manure into natural gas, encouraging the dairy sector to produce a gas we burn instead of one that just pollutes the air.
